title: Web3 Starter Pack (saved primer by Jay Drain Jr.)
html:
TL;DR

  • Saved third-party primer by Jay Drain Jr. (curated from "people 10x smarter than me") that Yahir clipped during his 2022 crypto onboarding phase
  • Defines the Web1/Web2/Web3 paradigm: read → read-write → read-write-own, with the Apple/Amazon/Facebook/Google centralization story as the Web2 critique
  • Covers blockchain mechanics (PoW/PoS/PoH consensus, smart contracts, nodes), ethereum as decentralized supercomputer, NFTs, DAOs, and Web3 social analogs (Showtime, Audius, Mirror, Bitclout, Filecoin, Arweave, Livepeer, The Graph, Decentraland)
  • Co-author network includes Spanish translator Daniel Zárate and Portuguese translator Tiago Yonamine — the doc was a community resource passed around in early-2022 Crypto Twitter circles
  • Same-month neighbor of 2021 04 27 crypto internship earning and 2022 04 05 ikigai blackbird — frames the era when Yahir was building the conceptual stack that would later route into OSIG and the FWEB3 wallet workshop

Notes

This is not a Yahir-authored doc — it's Jay Drain Jr.'s widely-circulated "web3 starter pack" that Yahir saved into his Drive in March 2022. The fact that it's in his Google Docs is itself the data: at this moment Yahir is teaching himself the field by clipping the canonical primers others have written, the same pattern as his later AI/SEO knowledge build.

Three things this doc seeds for the vault graph:

  1. The read-write-own framing of Web3 — which becomes the unstated operating principle behind Yahir's later interest in bromenu and Rankett as creator-owned data plays.
  2. The DYOR norm and the Chris Dixon / Packy McCormick / Ethereum.org reading-list that became the de-facto entry curriculum for the cohort.
  3. Confirmation Yahir was on Crypto Twitter following the right voices in early 2022 — a year before mainstream crypto retail collapsed and well before the LLM pivot.

Useful as a dated bookmark: when surveying what was Yahir reading in early 2022, this is the canonical answer.
